title: Graveyard Shift Takeover on Day-Only Legitimate User Pattern
narrative: Account shows 6+ months of consistent daytime activity (8am-6pm, avg transaction hour = 13.2) with typical purchases at grocery stores and gas stations averaging $47. Suddenly experiences 4-7 transactions between 3:17am-4:53am totaling $2,340-$3,890, all CNP digital goods (ProductCD='W') or gift cards. Legitimate user's historical is_night flag = 0 for 183 consecutive transactions, then flips to 1.0 for entire fraud sequence. Geographic mismatch shows dist1 jumping from consistent 0-12 miles to 847+ miles. Card velocity explodes: card1_txn_count_1hr goes from historical max of 1 to 6, card1_amt_zscore spikes to 4.8+. Fraudster maintains same card1/addr1 but switches P_emaildomain from user's consistent gmail.com to newly-seen protonmail.com or cock.li, suggesting credential compromise with email change during sleeping hours.
indicators: txn_hour between 3-5 for current transactions vs. historical mean txn_hour 11-15 over 180+ days
is_night flag = 1.0 for fraud sequence vs. 0.0 for 100% of prior 6-month transaction history
card1_txn_count_1hr jumps from historical maximum of 1 to 5-7 transactions
dist1 distance increases from consistent 0-12 mile pattern to 800+ miles during night window
TransactionAmt total $2,340-$3,890 in single night session vs. historical daily maximum $180
P_emaildomain switches from 6-month consistent domain to newly-seen temporary email provider
ProductCD shifts entirely to 'W' (digital goods) vs. historical 'C'/'H' (retail/physical) mix
summary: A card with 6+ months of consistent daytime activity (8am-6pm, typical $47 retail purchases) suddenly shows 4-7 transactions between 3-5am totaling $2,340-$3,890, all digital goods or gift cards, with the email domain changed to a temporary provider and geographic distance jumping from under 12 miles to 800+ miles.
reasoning: A fraudster has compromised the legitimate user's credentials and is exploiting them during sleeping hours when the victim won't notice—changing the email to maintain control, purchasing high-value digital goods that can be quickly monetized, and operating from a completely different location than the cardholder's established pattern.
indicator_explanations: Transactions happening in dead-of-night hours when cardholder normally shops midday
Activity occurs during nighttime when this card has never transacted before
Hourly transaction volume spikes far beyond any previous legitimate burst
Card suddenly appears hundreds of miles away during the overnight session
Single night spending exceeds months of typical daily purchase totals combined
Email switches from long-established domain to brand-new throwaway provider
Purchases flip to digital goods when cardholder only bought physical items
